name: Sourcegraph API
what_it_does: GraphQL API for Sourcegraph's code search and intelligence platform, enabling universal code search across repositories, code navigation, batch changes, and code insights.
use_cases: Searching across all repositories for patterns, usages, and definitions
Finding all call sites or references to a function across a large codebase
Automating large-scale code changes with Batch Changes API
Querying code insights and metrics for engineering leadership dashboards
Building AI coding tools that need semantic code understanding at scale
not_for: Teams without Sourcegraph instance (self-hosted or Sourcegraph.com)
Simple single-repo searches (use GitHub/GitLab search instead)
best_when: You have a large, multi-repository codebase and need to search, navigate, or make changes across it programmatically.
avoid_when: You're working in a single small repository or don't need cross-repository code intelligence.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens for user authentication. Service accounts for automation. Site admin tokens for admin operations. Token passed as Authorization Bearer header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sourcegraph.com: free for public repos; 10 private repos free
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise: custom pricing for self-hosted or cloud with unlimited repos
pricing.notes: Self-hosted open-source version available. Cloud subscription for managed service. Enterprise pricing for large organizations with SSO, batch changes, and insights.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by plan; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors follow standard spec with message and location. Custom error extensions provide error codes and context. Generally developer-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations fully idempotent. Batch changes have state management preventing duplicate execution.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL-only API — agents must construct and parse GraphQL queries, not simple REST calls
Search queries use Sourcegraph's own search syntax (regexp, structural) — not standard text search
Batch Changes (large-scale automated edits) require careful testing — can affect hundreds of repos
Code intelligence (go-to-definition, find references) requires LSIF/SCIP index upload per repo
Self-hosted instance required for private code — Sourcegraph.com only for public repos in free tier
GraphQL schema is large — recommend exploring with the GraphQL playground before building agents
